preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts for a WhatsApp Business channel derived from CLV-018 roadmap records.

acceptance_criteria.text: Define WhatsApp Business channel contracts for outbound messages, templates, inbound webhooks, media metadata, delivery statuses, disabled states, and credential validation.
Record provider prerequisites including business account, phone number ID, app secret, access token, webhook verify token, opt-in expectations, and template constraints before implementation ships.
Implement secure credential storage and webhook validation with no credential, token, contact, message, or media leakage through renderer, logs, diagnostics, exports, or screenshots.

Implement outbound and inbound text normalization behind the channel registry with deterministic tests for accepted send, rejected template, missing credentials, rate/quality limit, inbound duplicate, invalid signature, and delivery status update.
Add contact linking and policy enforcement so WhatsApp contacts do not receive trust, approval, urgent-interrupt, or unblock privileges without explicit operator configuration.
If interactive quick actions ship, require signed local action IDs, expiration, replay protection, template compliance, local pending-action lookup, and durable audit outcome records.
Add UI for provider setup, template/quality status, contact linking, disabled state, test send, and privacy/redaction preview.
Document setup burden, business-policy limitations, privacy limits, opt-in/opt-out behavior, failure modes, and shipped versus deferred features.
